source_image_dir: Folder of raw training images — each with a matching .txt caption sidecar. The caching step resizes these into post_image_dataset/resized/ and writes the VAE/text/PE caches under post_image_dataset/lora/. This is a preprocess-only knob: training never reads it (it reads the resized + cached outputs). Saved to configs/preprocess.toml; a preset/method file can still override it per-run, and a legacy copy left in base.toml still wins (backward compatible).
drop_lowres_images: When on, source images smaller than the 'Min pixels' threshold below are skipped during preprocessing so they never enter the resize / VAE / text caches — under-sized images add little signal and waste cache space. Turn off to keep every image regardless of size (equivalent to --min_pixels 0). Saved to configs/preprocess.toml.
min_pixels: Pixel-count threshold for the low-res filter (width × height). 500000 = 0.5MP, the default — matches the resize/caching scripts' own argparse defaults. Only applies when 'Drop low-resolution images' is on; otherwise it's ignored (the filter is disabled). Override per-run on the CLI with make preprocess ARGS="--min_pixels N". Saved to configs/preprocess.toml.
caption_shuffle_variants: Number of caption variants generated per image during text-encoder caching. v0 is the pristine original caption; v1..v(N-1) are smart-shuffled (the @artist prefix and 'On the …' / 'In the …' section anchors are preserved). The dataloader picks v0 with 20% probability and v1..v(N-1) uniformly otherwise — but only when use_shuffled_caption_variants=true in your method config. Set to 0 to cache a single pristine caption only.
caption_tag_dropout_rate: Per-tag dropout probability applied only to v1..v(N-1) shuffle variants. Tags up to and including the first @artist marker are never dropped — the artist tag is structurally important and the rating/character/series prefix is order-sensitive. Ignored when shuffle variants ≤ 0. Typical: 0.05–0.15. Higher values teach the LoRA to generalize across missing tags but can dilute the signal.
run_sam_mask: Whether to run SAM3 bubble segmentation during 'Run masking'. Uncheck to skip SAM entirely — useful when SAM produces false positives on your dataset or when you only want MIT's text detection. If both SAM and MIT are unchecked, the Run button is blocked.
run_mit_mask: Whether to run MIT/ComicTextDetector text segmentation during 'Run masking'. Uncheck to skip MIT — useful for non-manga datasets where its prior doesn't help, or to iterate faster on SAM-only configurations. If both SAM and MIT are unchecked, the Run button is blocked.
sam_prompts: Text prompts SAM3 will look for in each image. One prompt per line. Defaults are tuned for manga-style speech / text bubbles. Add custom prompts if your dataset has additional regions you want masked out (e.g. 'sound effect', 'caption box'). Saved to configs/sam_mask.yaml — read directly by preprocess/generate_masks.py.
sam_focus_prompts: The REVERSE of the prompts above. Normal prompts mask a region OUT (it's ignored in the loss, you train on everything else). Focus prompts name the subject to KEEP: the mask trains ONLY on what SAM3 detects here and ignores everything else — so 'girl' masks the entire background and the LoRA learns only the character. One prompt per line. Composes with the normal prompts in the same rule: the final trainable region is (focus subject) minus (those ignore regions), so you can focus the girl while still ignoring a speech bubble drawn over her. If SAM3 finds none of the focus subjects in an image, that image is left fully trainable rather than blanked out. Leave empty for the default ignore-only behaviour.
sam_rule_path_pattern: Which images THIS rule applies to — an fnmatch glob (matched against each image's path relative to post_image_dataset/resized), so different folders can get different mask types. 'character_a/*' targets one character; 'char_a/*|char_b/*' OR-combines several; '*portrait*' matches any path containing 'portrait'; empty or '*' is a catch-all that matches every image. Every rule whose pattern matches a given image COMPOSES on it (ignore-regions union together, focus-regions union together), so an ordered list of narrow rules plus one catch-all is a common layout. Distinct from the global 'mask path pattern' above, which scopes which images are masked at all (and is also forwarded to MIT).
sam_threshold: Minimum confidence required for a SAM3 detection to be kept. Range 0.0–1.0. Lower values produce more masks (with more false positives); higher values are stricter. Default 0.5. If SAM is missing real bubbles, lower this; if it's masking unrelated regions, raise it.
sam_dilate: Pixels of binary dilation applied to each SAM mask after thresholding. Larger values blur mask edges outward — useful when the underlying segmentation undershoots the actual text bubble border. Default 5. Set to 0 to disable dilation.
mit_text_threshold: Confidence threshold for the MIT/ComicTextDetector text segmenter. Range 0.0–1.0. Independent of SAM threshold — MIT uses a different model trained specifically on manga text regions. Default 0.8. Lower if MIT is missing text inside panels; raise if it's catching non-text artifacts.
mit_dilate: Pixels of binary dilation applied to each MIT mask. Same role as SAM dilate but tuned independently — MIT typically segments tight bounding regions around individual glyphs, so a moderate dilate value joins them into per-bubble blobs. Default 5.
mask_path_pattern: fnmatch glob restricting which resized images get masked, matched against each image's path relative to post_image_dataset/resized. Saved to configs/sam_mask.yaml; 'Run masking' reads it and forwards it to BOTH the SAM and MIT backends, so the two stay scoped identically. Same syntax as the training path_pattern: '*' (or blank) masks everything; 'char_a/*' restricts to one subfolder; 'char_a/*|char_b/*' OR-combines several; '*portrait*' matches any path containing 'portrait'. Use this to (re)generate masks for just one character/folder without rebuilding the whole mask tree.
target_res: Multi-scale constant-token resolution tiers, as a JSON list of edge sizes (allowed: 512, 768, 896, 1024, 1280, 1536). [1024] is the default — a single ~1MP tier, i.e. legacy single-resolution behavior. List more than one (e.g. [1024, 1536]) to train across resolutions like the v1.0 base model: during preprocessing each image is resized into the tier that resizes it the LEAST (nearest bucket by scale; one output per image), so a near-1MP image stays at 1024 rather than being downscaled to 768 and a mixed dataset spreads naturally across tiers. Token counts per tier: 512→1024(square)+1008, 768→2160, 896→3024+3000, 1024→4032+4200, 1280→6300, 1536→8640 — each forward runs at its real token count (no padding). Cost is compiled torch.compile block graphs: 768/1280/1536 add 1 each, 512/896 add 2 (they carry a near-square pair / square plus a diversity family), so keep the list short. The same value is read at train time only to size the torch.compile cache budget; training otherwise consumes whatever latent shapes are already cached on disk.
